event_preview:
The Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari is a 4.909 km (3.050 mi) permanent road course in Imola, 40 kilometres east of Bologna, featuring 21 corners across a compact layout where the seven-second gap in lap times between Hypercars and LMGT3 makes traffic management a defining strategic variable. With the Qatar 1812 km postponed until later in the campaign, the 6 Hours of Imola opens the 2026 FIA World Endurance Championship season. A 35-car field takes the start - 17 Hypercars and 18 LMGT3 entries representing 14 manufacturers - with Genesis making its WEC Hypercar debut via Mathieu Jaminet and Andre Lotterer. Ferrari arrives as defending double world champion and defending winner at this venue, the No. 51 crew of Pier Guidi, Calado and Giovinazzi having converted pole into victory here twelve months ago. Toyota arrives with a heavily updated TR010 Hybrid in its 100th WEC entry, chasing a potential 50th race win.

The all-time Hypercar Hyperpole record belongs to Giovinazzi, who turned a 1:28.920 in the No. 51 Ferrari in the 2025 Hyperpole session. The fastest Hypercar race lap on record is 1:32.504, set by Antonio Fuoco in the No. 50 Ferrari during the 2025 race. In LMGT3, the Hyperpole record belongs to Valentino Rossi, who set a 1:42.355 in the No. 46 Team WRT BMW in 2025.
